messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: AHP: Tooltips missing for some dropdowns ISSUE BODY: When using the CLI through AH, I don't get tooltips over any of these 4 icons: --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Toolbar Actions **New Item Dropdown** - Submenu in view title: - Add icon in navigation group - Submenu contains: New Agent, New Skill, New Instructions, New Prompt - Each opens PromptFilePickers to guide user through creation **Refresh** - ViewAction that calls `view.refresh()` **Collapse All** - ViewAction that calls `view.collapseAll()` All actions use `ViewAction` pattern and are gated by `when: view === AI_CUSTOMIZATION_VIEW_ID`. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## GitHub MCP Tools **Prefer using GitHub MCP tools over `gh` CLI commands** - these provide structured data and better integration: --- File: openproject_full.md --- ### Attribute help texts OpenProject offers Attribute help texts that provide additional information for attributes in work packages and projects, incl. custom fields. After setting them up in the Administration they are displayed when users click on the question mark symbol next to this specified attribute for projects and work packages. [Read more about Attribute help texts in OpenProject](../system-admin-guide/attribute-help-texts/). --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### Elements not appearing in snapshot - VS Code uses multiple webviews. Use `npx @playwright/cli tab-list` to list targets and switch to the right one with `npx @playwright/cli tab-select ` --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Tools Sources: built-in aliases, specific tools, MCP servers (`/*`), extension tools. **Special**: `[]` = no tools, omit = defaults. Body reference: `#tool:`

response:
```json
{
  "title": "AHP: Add tooltips to missing dropdown icons in toolbar",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["ui", "accessibility", "vscode-extension", "tooltip"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a VS Code user, I want tooltips to appear over all toolbar icons including dropdowns so that users can understand what each action does without hovering.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given the AI_CUSTOMIZATION_VIEW_ID is active, When hovering over any of the 4 missing icon/dropdown elements, Then a tooltip appears with descriptive text",
    "Given a user clicks on a dropdown icon, When the submenu opens, Then each menu item has its own tooltip when hovered",
    "The tooltips should appear within VS Code's native tooltip system (not custom webview tooltips)",
    "Tooltip content should be concise and informative for each action"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Identify the ViewAction implementations that are missing tooltip configuration",
      "Add tooltip property to affected actions in their definitions",
      "Verify tooltip metadata is properly serialized for webview communication"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update VS Code action definitions with tooltip strings for all 4 icons",
      "Test tooltips appear correctly on hover using Playwright",
      "Ensure tooltips render within VS Code's native UI layer"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Verify tooltips appear for all 4 missing icon locations",
      "Confirm tooltips are visible in different VS Code themes",
      "Test tooltip content accuracy and readability",
      "Validate tooltips work across multiple webviews if applicable"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "All 4 missing icons have tooltips configured",
    "Playwright tests pass for tooltip functionality",
    "Code reviewed by UI/UX team",
    "No regression in existing toolbar functionality"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Which are the exact 4 icons that need tooltips? The documentation only shows 3 toolbar actions (New Item Dropdown, Refresh, Collapse All).",
    "Are these tooltips expected to use VS Code's native tooltip system or custom webview tooltips?",
    "Should tooltips be shown for all views or specifically AI_CUSTOMIZATION_VIEW_ID?",
    "What is the preferred format for tooltip content - simple string or HTML?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The missing icons are part of the toolbar actions defined in ViewAction<AICustomizationViewPane>",
    "Tooltip configuration follows VS Code's action definition pattern",
    "This affects only the AI Helper extension (AHP)",
    "The existing tooltip implementation for other elements can be used as reference"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Part 1: Identify missing tooltips",
      "description": "Audit all toolbar actions to find which ones lack tooltip configuration"
    },
    {
      "name": "Part 2: Add tooltip definitions",
      "description": "Implement tooltip strings for the identified missing icons"
    },
    {
      "name": "Part 3: Testing and validation",
      "description": "QA verification across different scenarios and themes"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Toolbar Actions section",
    "openproject_full.md - Attribute help texts reference",
    "Issue body with screenshot description"
  ]
}
```
